title: rosa woodsii f. hispida
intro: Rosa woodsii f. hispida, also known as the Rocky Mountain rose or woods' rose, is a subspecies of flowering shrub native to the western United States and Canada. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as to treat digestive issues and skin irritations. Rosa woodsii f. hispida can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
